steps.description: Write cosecant as one over sine
steps.explanation: Converting cosecant into a sine expression lets us use the exact values we already know from the special triangles.

steps.description: Recall the exact value of sine 30 degrees
steps.explanation: This exact value comes from the $30$-$60$-$90$ triangle and is one of the standard results to memorise.
Replacing sine with a half reduces the problem to a reciprocal of a simple fraction.
The reciprocal of one half is two, so the calculation resolves cleanly to a whole number.
Cosecant is always at least $1$ in magnitude, and $2$ meets this requirement, confirming the result is plausible.
This is the exact answer, needing no decimal approximation.
